primary_meaning: in or to a foreign country
usage_notes: Use with 'go', 'travel', 'be' - go abroad, travel abroad
common_collocations: go abroad, travel abroad, live abroad, study abroad
image_search_query: airplane flying internationally or passport with stamps
image_context: A plane flying high above clouds or a passport with multiple entry stamps from different countries
example_1: She decided to study abroad in France.
example_1_translation: Ela decidiu estudar no exterior na França.
example_2: Many people go abroad for work.
example_2_translation: Muitas pessoas vão para o exterior para trabalhar.
example_3: He's been living abroad for five years.
example_3_translation: Ele está morando no exterior há cinco anos.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: a-BROAD. Rhymes with 'road' and 'load'

primary_meaning: in or to a country across the sea
usage_notes: Similar to 'abroad', often used with 'live', 'work', 'study'
common_collocations: live overseas, work overseas, go overseas, overseas students
image_search_query: map showing overseas destinations or beach resort overseas
image_context: A world map with arrows pointing across oceans to different countries, or a tropical beach resort showing overseas travel
example_1: He met her while working overseas.
example_1_translation: Ele a conheceu enquanto trabalhava no exterior.
example_2: Many overseas students attend this university.
example_2_translation: Muitos estudantes estrangeiros frequentam esta universidade.
example_3: We're planning a trip overseas next summer.
example_3_translation: Estamos planejando uma viagem ao exterior no próximo verão.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: o-ver-SEAS. Emphasize 'seas': o-ver-SEAS
memory_hook: Overseas - over the 'seas' - across the ocean

usage_notes: Used to introduce a contrast. Same meaning as 'though'
common_collocations: although it, although she, although they, even although
image_search_query: person thinking while holding two contrasting ideas
image_context: A person holding two puzzle pieces that don't quite fit together, showing contrast
example_1: Although it was raining, we went for a walk.
example_1_translation: Embora estivesse chovendo, fomos passear.
example_2: She's very kind, although she doesn't show her feelings.
example_2_translation: Ela é muito gentil, embora não mostre seus sentimentos.
example_3: Although I was tired, I couldn't sleep.
example_3_translation: Embora eu estivesse cansado, não conseguia dormir.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: al-THOUGH. Emphasize 'though'

usage_notes: Used to introduce a contrast or unexpected result. Place after first clause
common_collocations: however, however it, however she, however they
image_search_query: road sign showing however or sign with opposite directions
image_context: A road sign with arrows pointing in opposite directions, or a person shrugging with palms up showing uncertainty
example_1: I wanted to go; however, I was too tired.
example_1_translation: Eu queria ir; no entanto, estava cansado demais.
example_2: The weather was bad. However, we enjoyed our trip.
example_2_translation: O clima estava ruim. No entanto, aproveitamos a viagem.
example_3: She doesn't like sweets. However, she ate the cake.
example_3_translation: Ela não gosta de doces. No entanto, ela comeu o bolo.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: how-EV-er. Emphasize 'ever'
memory_hook: How-ever - in whatever way it happens

usage_notes: Used to express probability. Placed before main verb, after auxiliary
common_collocations: probably will, probably is, probably are, probably did
image_search_query: weather forecast showing probability or dice with chance
image_context: A weather forecast showing probability percentages, or dice showing chance and likelihood
example_1: It will probably rain tomorrow.
example_1_translation: Provavelmente vai chover amanhã.
example_2: She's probably at home by now.
example_2_translation: Ela provavelmente está em casa agora.
example_3: We'll probably go to the beach this weekend.
example_3_translation: Provavelmente vamos à praia neste fim de semana.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: PRO-ba-bly. Emphasize first: PRO-ba-bly

usage_notes: Used to express uncertainty. Can go at beginning or before verb
common_collocations: maybe it, maybe you, maybe we, maybe later
image_search_query: person shrugging shoulders with uncertain expression
image_context: A person with arms raised and palms up in a shrugging gesture, expressing uncertainty
example_1: Maybe we'll go to the movies tonight.
example_1_translation: Talvez vamos ao cinema esta noite.
example_2: Is she coming? Maybe, I'm not sure.
example_2_translation: Ela está vindo? Talvez, não tenho certeza.
example_3: Maybe you should try calling her.
example_3_translation: Talvez você deveria tentar ligar para ela.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: MAY-be. Emphasize first: MAY-be
common_mistakes: 'Maybe' not 'may be' - one word when meaning perhaps

usage_notes: Opposite is 'later', 'eventually'. Used for urgent actions
common_collocations: do immediately, come immediately, stop immediately
synonyms: at once, right away, instantly, now
image_search_query: red light or stop sign representing immediate action
image_context: A bright red emergency stop button or a red light, representing immediate action required
example_1: Please respond immediately to this email.
example_1_translation: Por favor, responda imediatamente a este email.
example_2: He left immediately after the meeting.
example_2_translation: Ele saiu imediatamente após a reunião.
example_3: Call me immediately if anything changes.
example_3_translation: Ligue para mim imediatamente se algo mudar.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: im-ME-di-ate-ly. Emphasize second: im-ME-di-ate-ly
related_words: now, at once, right away, instantly

usage_notes: Used to describe unexpected events or quick changes
common_collocations: suddenly appeared, suddenly stopped, suddenly realized, suddenly began
image_search_query: lightning bolt or surprise pop-up representing sudden change
image_context: A lightning bolt striking suddenly or a jack-in-the-box popping out unexpectedly
example_1: The lights suddenly went out during the movie.
example_1_translation: As luzes de repente se apagaram durante o filme.
example_2: She suddenly started laughing for no reason.
example_2_translation: Ela de repente começou a rir sem motivo.
example_3: I suddenly realized I had forgotten my keys.
example_3_translation: De repente percebi que tinha esquecido minhas chaves.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: SUD-den-ly. Emphasize first: SUD-den-ly
related_words: unexpectedly, abruptly, all at once, quickly

usage_notes: Opposite of 'suddenly'. Used for changes that happen slowly
common_collocations: gradually improved, gradually changed, gradually increased, gradually decreased
image_search_query: ramp or slope representing gradual change
image_context: A wheelchair ramp or gentle slope rising gradually, showing slow progression
example_1: The weather gradually improved throughout the day.
example_1_translation: O clima melhorou gradualmente ao longo do dia.
example_2: She gradually got used to the new schedule.
example_2_translation: Ela foi se acostumando gradualmente com o novo horário.
example_3: The city has gradually grown over the years.
example_3_translation: A cidade foi crescendo gradualmente ao longo dos anos.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: GRA-du-al-ly. Emphasize first: GRA-du-al-ly
memory_hook: Gradually - from 'grade' -一步一步 (step by step)
related_words: slowly, progressively, step by step, eventually

primary_meaning: in the end, after a long time
usage_notes: Used when something happens after a long process or delay
common_collocations: eventually found, eventually decided, eventually became, eventually succeeded
synonyms: finally, in the end, after all, at last
image_search_query: light at end of tunnel or marathon runner approaching finish
image_context: A light at the end of a long tunnel or a runner approaching the finish line after a long race
example_1: After many tries, she eventually passed the exam.
example_1_translation: Depois de muitas tentativas, ela finalmente passou no exame.
example_2: He eventually realized his mistake.
example_2_translation: Ele eventualmente percebeu seu erro.
example_3: We eventually arrived at the hotel after the long drive.
example_3_translation: Eventually chegamos ao hotel após a longa viagem.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: e-VEN-tual-ly. Emphasize second: e-VEN-tual-ly
related_words: finally, in the end, after all, at last

usage_notes: Used to emphasize truth or to correct something
common_collocations: actually I, actually it, actually not, actually yes
synonyms: really, in fact, indeed, as a matter of fact
image_search_query: person with surprised expression or truth reveal
image_context: A person with a surprised 'aha' expression or cards being revealed showing the truth
example_1: I actually don't like coffee at all.
example_1_translation: Na verdade, eu não gosto nada de café.
example_2: That's actually a very good idea.
example_2_translation: Isso é realmente uma muito boa ideia.
example_3: Did she actually say that?
example_3_translation: Ela realmente disse isso?
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: AC-tual-ly. Emphasize first: AC-tual-ly

usage_notes: Used for emphasis. Stronger than 'very'
common_collocations: completely different, completely new, completely wrong, completely agree
image_search_query: full circle or completely filled container
image_context: A pie chart that is 100% filled or a glass completely full of water
example_1: I'm completely tired after that long trip.
example_1_translation: Estou completamente cansado após aquela longa viagem.
example_2: The two ideas are completely different.
example_2_translation: As duas ideias são completamente diferentes.
example_3: I completely forgot about the meeting.
example_3_translation: Esqueci completamente da reunião.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: com-PLETE-ly. Emphasize middle: com-PLETE-ly

usage_notes: Used to express certainty. Stronger than 'probably'
common_collocations: definitely will, definitely is, definitely not, most definitely
image_search_query: checkmark or thumbs up representing certainty
image_context: A large green checkmark on a white background or a firm thumbs up gesture
example_1: I'll definitely be there at nine o'clock.
example_1_translation: Definitivamente estarei lá às nove horas.
example_2: That's definitely the best restaurant in town.
example_2_translation: Esse é definitivamente o melhor restaurante da cidade.
example_3: Are you coming? Definitely!
example_3_translation: Você está vindo? Definitivamente!
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: DEF-i-nite-ly. Emphasize first: DEF-i-nite-ly

usage_notes: Very strong emphasis. Often used alone to agree strongly
common_collocations: absolutely right, absolutely wrong, absolutely love, absolutely fantastic
image_search_query: 100% sign or completely full meter
image_context: A 100% progress bar completely filled or an 'ABSOLUTE' stamp in red ink
example_1: That's absolutely impossible!
example_1_translation: Isso é absolutamente impossível!
example_2: I absolutely love this movie.
example_2_translation: Eu amo absolutamente este filme.
example_3: Do you agree? Absolutely!
example_3_translation: Você concorda? Absolutamente!
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: ab-so-LUTE-ly. Emphasize third: ab-so-LUTE-ly

usage_notes: Used to emphasize something special or important
common_collocations: especially important, especially today, especially if, especially when
image_search_query: star highlighting special item or spotlight
image_context: A gold star with a spotlight shining on it or a highlighted section in a document
example_1: I love all fruits, especially strawberries.
example_1_translation: Eu amo todas as frutas, especialmente morangos.
example_2: It's especially important to arrive on time.
example_2_translation: É especialmente importante chegar na hora.
example_3: She was tired, especially after the long meeting.
example_3_translation: Ela estava cansada, especialmente após a longa reunião.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: es-PE-cial-ly. Emphasize second: es-PE-cial-ly
memory_hook: Es-pe-cially - from 'special' - in a special way

usage_notes: Often used with 'on a journey', 'make a journey'
common_collocations: make a journey, long journey, journey to, journey home
image_search_query: winding road or person hiking on a journey path
image_context: A long winding mountain road or a backpacker hiking along a scenic trail journey
example_1: The journey from London to Edinburgh takes about four hours.
example_1_translation: A viagem de Londres a Edimburgo leva cerca de quatro horas.
example_2: It was a long and difficult journey.
example_2_translation: Foi uma longa e difícil jornada.
example_3: Life is a journey full of surprises.
example_3_translation: A vida é uma jornada cheia de surpresas.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: JOUR-ney. Emphasize first: JOUR-ney

usage_notes: Often used with 'heavy', 'light', 'check in luggage'
common_collocations: heavy luggage, check luggage, luggage rack, luggage allowance
image_search_query: suitcases and bags on luggage carousel at airport
image_context: Colorful suitcases and bags on an airport luggage carousel or luggage conveyor belt
example_1: We have too much luggage for the plane.
example_1_translation: Temos muita bagagem para o avião.
example_2: Please put your luggage in the overhead compartment.
example_2_translation: Por favor, coloque sua bagagem no compartimento superior.
example_3: I lost my luggage during the flight connection.
example_3_translation: Perdi minha bagagem durante a conexão do voo.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: LUG-gage. Emphasize first: LUG-gage

primary_meaning: a person or company that pays people to work
usage_notes: Opposite is 'employee'. The person who hires
common_collocations: good employer, employer benefits, potential employer
image_search_query: business person in suit or company building
image_context: A business person in a formal suit shaking hands or a company headquarters building
example_1: My employer offers good health insurance.
example_1_translation: Meu empregador oferece bom seguro de saúde.
example_2: She's looking for a new employer.
example_2_translation: Ela está procurando um novo empregador.
example_3: The employer met with all the new staff.
example_3_translation: O empregador conheceu toda a nova equipe.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: em-PLOY-er. Emphasize second: em-PLOY-er
common_mistakes: 'Employer' not 'employer' - the one who employs, not who is employed

primary_meaning: a person who works for a company
usage_notes: Opposite is 'employer'. Someone who is hired
common_collocations: new employee, loyal employee, full-time employee, employee benefits
image_search_query: worker at desk or person wearing employee badge
image_context: A person working at a desk with a company ID badge or a uniform showing they work for a company
example_1: The company has over 500 employees.
example_1_translation: A empresa tem mais de 500 funcionários.
example_2: She's a new employee in the marketing department.
example_2_translation: Ela é uma nova funcionária do departamento de marketing.
example_3: Employee satisfaction is very important to us.
example_3_translation: A satisfação do funcionário é muito importante para nós.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: em-PLOY-ee. Emphasize second: em-PLOY-ee

usage_notes: Usually for professional relationships
common_collocations: close colleague, colleague from, work colleague
image_search_query: two professionals working together in office
image_context: Two professionals in an office discussing work or collaborating at a shared desk
example_1: She's my closest colleague at work.
example_1_translation: Ela é minha colega mais próxima no trabalho.
example_2: I had lunch with a colleague today.
example_2_translation: Almocei com um colega hoje.
example_3: My colleagues helped me a lot with the project.
example_3_translation: Meus colegas me ajudaram muito com o projeto.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: COL-league. 'Col' like 'call', 'league' like 'league'

primary_meaning: very bad weather with wind and rain
usage_notes: Often combined: thunderstorm, rainstorm, snowstorm
common_collocations: heavy storm, thunderstorm, before the storm, storm warning
image_search_query: dark storm clouds or lightning during thunderstorm
image_context: Dark cumulonimbus clouds with lightning bolts during a dramatic thunderstorm
example_1: A big storm is coming this weekend.
example_1_translation: Uma grande tempestade está vindo neste fim de semana.
example_2: The storm damaged many houses in the village.
example_2_translation: A tempestade danificou muitas casas na vila.
example_3: We had to cancel the picnic because of the storm.
example_3_translation: Tivemos que cancelar o piknik por causa da tempestade.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable: STORM. Rhymes with 'form' and 'warm'
memory_hook: Storm - sounds like the noise it makes

primary_meaning: how hot or cold something is
usage_notes: Used with 'high', 'low', 'rise', 'fall', 'normal'
common_collocations: high temperature, low temperature, temperature rise, body temperature
image_search_query: thermometer showing temperature or weather gauge
image_context: A classic thermometer showing different temperature levels or a digital temperature display
example_1: What's the temperature today?
example_1_translation: Qual é a temperatura hoje?
example_2: The temperature dropped to minus ten degrees last night.
example_2_translation: A temperatura caiu para menos dez graus anoche.
example_3: She has a high temperature and needs to rest.
example_3_translation: Ela está com febre e precisa descansar.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: tem-per-a-TURE. Emphasize third: tem-per-a-TURE

usage_notes: Often 'weather forecast'. Also used in other contexts
common_collocations: weather forecast, five-day forecast, forecast for tomorrow
image_search_query: weather forecast on TV or weather app on phone
image_context: A weather anchor presenting a forecast on TV or a smartphone showing a weather forecast app
example_1: The weather forecast says it'll rain tomorrow.
example_1_translation: A previsão do tempo diz que vai chover amanhã.
example_2: Let's check the five-day forecast before we plan the trip.
example_2_translation: Vamos verificar a previsão de cinco dias antes de planejar a viagem.
example_3: The sales forecast looks good for next quarter.
example_3_translation: A previsão de vendas parece boa para o próximo trimestre.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: FORE-cast. Emphasize first: FORE-cast

usage_notes: Use with 'on the internet', 'via the internet'
common_collocations: on the internet, internet connection, internet access, use the internet
image_search_query: globe with network lines or wifi symbol
image_context: A blue globe with glowing network connection lines or a WiFi symbol
example_1: I found the information on the internet.
example_1_translation: Encontrei a informação na internet.
example_2: The internet connection is slow today.
example_2_translation: A conexão com a internet está lenta hoje.
example_3: More and more people shop on the internet.
example_3_translation: Cada vez mais pessoas compram na internet.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: IN-ter-net. Emphasize first: IN-ter-net

usage_notes: Opposite is 'hardware'. Programs you install
common_collocations: software update, software download, install software, software company
image_search_query: computer programs icons or download progress bar
image_context: A computer screen showing multiple program icons or a download progress bar for software
example_1: I need to update my software.
example_1_translation: Preciso atualizar meu software.
example_2: This software helps me manage my schedule.
example_2_translation: Este software me ajuda a gerenciar minha agenda.
example_3: The software is available for free download.
example_3_translation: O software está disponível para download gratuito.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: SOFT-ware. Emphasize first: SOFT-ware
memory_hook: Soft-ware - 'soft' part of computer - opposite of 'hard'ware

primary_meaning: the circumstances at a particular time
usage_notes: Use with 'in', 'on', 'a difficult situation'
common_collocations: difficult situation, current situation, situation improve, in this situation
image_search_query: person thinking at crossroads or puzzle pieces
image_context: A person standing at a crossroads or multiple puzzle pieces representing different situations
example_1: The situation is getting better.
example_1_translation: A situação está melhorando.
example_2: I'm in a difficult financial situation.
example_2_translation: Estou em uma situação financeira difícil.
example_3: How would you handle this situation?
example_3_translation: Como você lidaria com esta situação?
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: si-tua-TION. Emphasize third: si-tua-TION

primary_meaning: the skill or power to do something
usage_notes: Use with 'have the ability to', 'natural ability'
common_collocations: ability to, natural ability, special ability, have the ability
image_search_query: person showing special skill or superhero with power
image_context: A person demonstrating a special skill like playing an instrument, or a superhero displaying their power
example_1: She has the ability to learn languages quickly.
example_1_translation: Ela tem a habilidade de aprender idiomas rapidamente.
example_2: His musical ability is amazing.
example_2_translation: A habilidade musical dele é incrível.
example_3: We test each candidate's abilities before hiring.
example_3_translation: Testamos as habilidades de cada candidato antes de contratar.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: a-BIL-i-ty. Emphasize second: a-BIL-i-ty
memory_hook: A-bil-i-ty - from 'able' - the state of being able

primary_meaning: an option between two or more things
usage_notes: Use with 'make a choice', 'have a choice', 'no choice'
common_collocations: make a choice, have a choice, difficult choice, many choices
image_search_query: multiple options or person at fork in road
image_context: Multiple arrows pointing in different directions or a person standing at a fork in the road
example_1: I have to make a difficult choice.
example_1_translation: Tenho que fazer uma escolha difícil.
example_2: You have two choices: stay or leave.
example_2_translation: Você tem duas escolhas: ficar ou partir.
example_3: She didn't have much choice in the matter.
example_3_translation: Ela não teve muita escolha no assunto.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable: CHOICE. Rhymes with 'voice' and 'ice'
memory_hook: Choice - choose with 'oice' - the result of choosing

usage_notes: Use with 'tell the truth', 'the truth is', 'in truth'
common_collocations: tell the truth, the truth is, in truth, search for truth
image_search_query: scales of justice or person revealing truth
image_context: Scales of justice balanced perfectly or a person holding a glowing light representing truth
example_1: I always tell the truth.
example_1_translation: Eu sempre digo a verdade.
example_2: Is she telling the truth?
example_2_translation: Ela está dizendo a verdade?
example_3: The truth is, I was too afraid to ask.
example_3_translation: A verdade é que eu estava com medo demais para perguntar.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable: TRUTH. Rhymes with 'tooth' and 'smooth'
memory_hook: Truth - the 'r' is there - like 'trust' without the 's'

usage_notes: Can be countable (values/principles) or uncountable (worth)
common_collocations: good value, value of, values and beliefs, add value
image_search_query: gold coin or price tag showing value
image_context: A gold coin, price tag showing value, or a heart with values written around it
example_1: The value of this car has increased.
example_1_translation: O valor deste carro aumentou.
example_2: This book is of great value to me.
example_2_translation: Este livro é de grande valor para mim.
example_3: Family values are important in our culture.
example_3_translation: Valores familiares são importantes em nossa cultura.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: VAL-ue. Emphasize first: VAL-ue
